source.book: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析6》

segments.text: Jeannie: Hi Dan.
segments.translation: 嗨，Dan。

segments.text: Jeannie: How's it going?
segments.translation: 最近怎么样？

segments.text: Dan: Hello Jeannie.
segments.translation: 你好，Jeannie。

segments.text: Dan: Pretty well, thanks.
segments.translation: 挺好的，谢谢。

segments.text: Dan: Have you finished your research yet?
segments.translation: 你的研究做完了吗？

segments.text: Jeannie: Yes, it's all done.
segments.translation: 做完了，全都搞定了。

segments.text: Jeannie: I've spent several weeks on it, from the very beginning of deciding the topic, to carrying out the research, and writing the essay.
segments.translation: 我在上面花了好几个星期，从一开始定题目，到实际做研究，再到写论文。

segments.text: Dan: Congratulations!
segments.translation: 恭喜你！

segments.text: Dan: It seems like a big project.
segments.translation: 看起来是个不小的工程。

segments.text: Dan: I know the research is to do with music, but what is it exactly about?
segments.translation: 我知道你的研究跟音乐有关，但具体是研究什么的？

segments.text: Jeannie: Well, at first I didn't have a specific purpose until one day I read a report that said 'if appropriate music is played while people are eating, it may cause some changes in their behaviour'.
segments.translation: 嗯，一开始我并没有明确的方向，直到有一天我读到一份报告，上面说“如果人们用餐时播放合适的音乐，可能会让他们的行为发生一些变化”。

segments.text: Jeannie: But the report didn't illustrate the changes in detail, so I wanted to carry out a thorough investigation into this.
segments.translation: 但那份报告没有详细说明具体是哪些变化，所以我就想对这个问题做一次深入的调查。

segments.text: Dan: That's quite unique.
segments.translation: 这挺特别的。

segments.text: Dan: So, how did you conduct your research?
segments.translation: 那你是怎么开展研究的呢？

segments.text: Jeannie: I started by looking for five different restaurants, and then went to each of the restaurants to order some food and observe other people.
segments.translation: 我先找了五家不同的餐厅，然后分别去每家餐厅点些吃的，同时观察其他顾客。

segments.text: Jeannie: I mainly focused on how the music is played instead of how people eat.
segments.translation: 我主要关注的是音乐是怎么播放的，而不是人们怎么吃饭。

segments.text: Jeannie: Findings of my observations indicate that the five restaurants have something in common – they all have a special section to play the music.
segments.translation: 我的观察结果表明，这五家餐厅有一个共同点——它们都有一个专门放音乐的区域。

segments.text: Jeannie: More importantly, although the music is played at different times, the volume is unchanged.
segments.translation: 更重要的是，虽然音乐是在不同时段播放的，但音量是不变的。

segments.text: Dan: Sounds attractive.
segments.translation: 听起来很吸引人。

segments.text: Dan: I've never read anything about this before.
segments.translation: 我以前从没读到过关于这方面的东西。

segments.text: Dan: In addition to observation, what other research methods have you used?
segments.translation: 除了观察之外，你还用了什么别的研究方法？

segments.text: Jeannie: I also distributed the survey questionnaire to people dining there.
segments.translation: 我还给在那儿用餐的人发放了调查问卷。

segments.text: Dan: What questions are posed in your questionnaire?
segments.translation: 你的问卷里都问了些什么问题？

segments.text: Jeannie: Well, the first section of the questionnaire asks subjects to indicate the number of times they have been to the restaurant previously, the number of people they dine with and whether they think they will return to the restaurant.
segments.translation: 嗯，问卷的第一部分是让受访者填写他们之前来这家餐厅的次数、一起用餐的人数，以及他们觉得自己会不会再来。

segments.text: Jeannie: These early questions don't involve musical preferences, but largely act as 'distracters' – the intention being that subjects would be less inclined to think that the survey is about the music being played.
segments.translation: 这些开头的问题跟音乐偏好没关系，主要是起“干扰项”的作用——目的是让受访者不太会想到这份问卷其实是关于所播放的音乐的。

segments.text: Dan: That sounds very reasonable.
segments.translation: 这听起来很合理。

segments.text: Dan: Did you design the questionnaire by yourself?
segments.translation: 问卷是你自己设计的吗？

segments.text: Jeannie: Actually no, I read an assignment a student wrote last year, which was just kept beside a dictionary in the library.
segments.translation: 其实不是，我看了一份去年一个学生写的作业，它就放在图书馆一本词典旁边。

segments.text: Jeannie: I came across it when I was looking something up in the dictionary.
segments.translation: 我在词典里查东西的时候正好碰到了它。

segments.text: Dan: Oh, you were so lucky.
segments.translation: 哦，你运气真好。

segments.text: Dan: Is there any other question included in the questionnaire?
segments.translation: 问卷里还有别的问题吗？

segments.text: Jeannie: Yes, subjects are also asked why they choose to dine at a particular restaurant.
segments.translation: 有的，还会问受访者为什么选择在某家特定的餐厅用餐。

segments.text: Jeannie: Several reasons are listed in the questionnaire.
segments.translation: 问卷里列出了好几个原因。

segments.text: Jeannie: I thought people would put service first, but that's not the case.
segments.translation: 我本以为大家会把服务放在第一位，但结果并非如此。

segments.text: Jeannie: Only about 12% of them have chosen this reason, while the majority of respondents think whether they will pick this restaurant depends on how many available seats it has.
segments.translation: 只有大约12%的人选了这个原因，而大多数受访者认为，会不会选这家餐厅取决于它有多少空位。

segments.text: Jeannie: They think it's terrible to eat in a crowded place.
segments.translation: 他们觉得在拥挤的地方吃饭很糟糕。

segments.text: Dan: Yes, everyone wants to enjoy a meal comfortably.
segments.translation: 是啊，谁都想舒舒服服地吃顿饭。

segments.text: Dan: Then I guess maybe the location of a restaurant is an important factor since no one likes to waste too much time on the way?
segments.translation: 那我猜，餐厅的位置可能也是个重要因素吧，毕竟没人喜欢在路上浪费太多时间？

segments.text: Jeannie: It used to be, but nowadays nearly every household has a private car, so they don't have to be worried about the traffic problem.
segments.translation: 以前是这样，但现在几乎家家都有私家车，所以他们不用太担心交通问题。

segments.text: Jeannie: What I find interesting, however, is that a large percentage of people say they will compare different restaurants in the neighbourhood on the Internet first.
segments.translation: 不过我觉得有意思的是，很大一部分人说他们会先在网上比较一下附近的不同餐厅。

segments.text: Jeannie: If one of them stands out from its rivals, they will choose it.
segments.translation: 如果其中某一家比同类餐厅更出众，他们就会选它。

segments.text: Dan: Wow, it's really interesting.
segments.translation: 哇，这真的挺有意思的。

segments.text: Dan: What else then?
segments.translation: 那还有别的吗？

segments.text: Jeannie: Well, the second section of the questionnaire asks subjects to respond to the music being played by rating so that the relationship between music and perceived atmosphere could be examined directly.
segments.translation: 嗯，问卷的第二部分是让受访者给所播放的音乐打分做出反应，这样就能直接考察音乐和感受到的氛围之间的关系。

segments.text: Jeannie: As a means of examining purchase intention, the final section of the questionnaire asks them to indicate the maximum amount of money they are prepared to spend on their main meal.
segments.translation: 为了考察购买意愿，问卷的最后一部分是让他们填写自己愿意为主餐花的最高金额。

segments.text: Dan: So what have you finally discovered?
segments.translation: 那你最终发现了什么？

segments.text: Jeannie: I collected all the questionnaires and analysed people's answers to get a clear idea of their reactions to different music conditions.
segments.translation: 我把所有问卷都收集起来，分析了大家的回答，从而清楚地了解他们对不同音乐环境的反应。

segments.text: Jeannie: The result shows that music has become an indispensable part of restaurants.
segments.translation: 结果显示，音乐已经成了餐厅不可或缺的一部分。

segments.text: Jeannie: If people find there isn't any music during the meal, they will think the restaurant charges too much.
segments.translation: 如果人们发现用餐时没有任何音乐，他们就会觉得这家餐厅收费太高了。

segments.text: Dan: Yes, now music is everywhere in our life.
segments.translation: 是啊，如今音乐在我们生活中无处不在。

segments.text: Dan: Is there any difference in people's attitudes towards different musical styles?
segments.translation: 人们对不同音乐风格的态度有区别吗？

segments.text: Jeannie: Yes.
segments.translation: 有的。

segments.text: Jeannie: Most people think that if jazz is being played when eating, they tend to stay at the restaurant longer and order more food to enjoy the music.
segments.translation: 大多数人觉得，吃饭时如果放的是爵士乐，他们往往会在餐厅待得更久，还会多点些吃的，好一边享受音乐。

segments.text: Jeannie: But this is quite the opposite of what happens when classical music is played.
segments.translation: 但放古典音乐时的情况恰恰相反。

segments.text: Jeannie: Many customers think if the restaurant plays classical music, they wouldn't like to stay there for another minute, but to finish the food quickly and hurry away, because they don't like this type of music at all.
segments.translation: 很多顾客觉得，如果餐厅放古典音乐，他们一分钟都不想多待，只想赶紧把饭吃完然后走人，因为他们根本不喜欢这种音乐。

segments.text: Jeannie: And lastly, about pop music, it's very popular these days, especially among young people, as I've noticed in restaurants.
segments.translation: 最后是流行音乐，正如我在餐厅里注意到的，这种音乐现在很受欢迎，尤其是在年轻人中间。

segments.text: Jeannie: They like listening to this type of music during their meals.
segments.translation: 他们喜欢在用餐时听这种音乐。

segments.text: Jeannie: As a result, this greatly increases the possibility of their presence at the same restaurant.
segments.translation: 因此，这大大提高了他们再次光顾同一家餐厅的可能性。

segments.text: Dan: Great.
segments.translation: 太棒了。

segments.text: Dan: I've learned a lot from your research.
segments.translation: 我从你的研究里学到了很多。

segments.text: Dan: I think it's meaningful and innovative.
segments.translation: 我觉得它既有意义又有新意。

segments.text: Dan: Thanks for telling me, Jeannie.
segments.translation: 谢谢你跟我分享，Jeannie。

segments.text: Jeannie: You are welcome, Dan.
segments.translation: 不客气，Dan。
